title: Thelychiton adae
taxonomy: Thelychiton adae is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Hymenophyllaceae. This plant is native to Australia and is a type of fern. It is a part of the genus Thelychiton, which was previously known as Diphylleia. The classification of Thelychiton adae is further divided into the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Marchantiophyta, which is a division of non-vascular plants. However, some sources may place Thelychiton adae in the division Pteridophyta, which consists of vascular plants such as ferns.

intro: Thelychiton adae, also known as the Queensland arrowroot, is a plant species native to Australia, belonging to the family Hanguanaceae. The Queensland arrowroot is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in the treatment of skin conditions, wounds, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Thelychiton adae include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
